title: Measure PSF peak and FWHM
instruction: Implement measure_psf in psf.py. Given a 1D intensity profile (point-spread-like), return (peak_value, fwhm) where peak_value is the maximum intensity and fwhm is the full width at half maximum in sample units. Use linear interpolation between samples for the half-max crossings. If the peak is non-positive or the profile never falls to half max on both sides, return (peak_value, 0.0). Empty input returns (0.0, 0.0). Inspired by common computational-imaging calibration checks (peak/FWHM of a PSF slice), but fully self-contained with no external datasets.
difficulty_rationale: Requires correct half-max search and interpolation; easy to get off-by-one or wrong baseline handling.

provenance.source: inspired by computational imaging PSF calibration metrics (e.g. ImagingBench-style optics tasks); not a direct copy of any external item
